meaning: n.折磨,痛苦,拷问,拷打vt.拷问,曲解,折磨,使弯曲
examples: Many of the refugees have suffered torture.
He would not torture her further by trying to argue with her.
I believed that in civilized countries, torture had ended long ago.
The U.S. Justice Department publicly condemned the use of torture during interrogation in 2004.
It's not very different from trying to comprehend a foreign language, which is torture to dyslexics.
